Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 
28th April 2026  

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East 
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.
  
● UN Secretary-General António Guterres appealed to all parties to reopen the Strait of Hormuz without tolls or discrimination to restore freedom of navigation.

● UAE announced its exit from OPEC effective May 1, delivering a significant blow to the cartel amid ongoing oil market disruptions from the US-Iran conflict.

● At a UNSC maritime security meeting, India called for the immediate restoration of freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

● A Japanese-linked VLCC, IDEMITSU MARU, was transiting the Strait of Hormuz outbound from Persian Gulf. It was carrying approx. 2 million barrels of Saudi crude via the designated lanes.

● Panama-flagged, Chinese-owned product tanker STARWAY and Chinese-flagged bulk carrier LAI ZHOU 66 transited the Strait of Hormuz today.

● Pakistan has opened six overland routes with Iran to facilitate trade and bypass the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

● The IDF announced it dismantled over 1,000 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in southern Lebanon and seized weapons including machine guns, Kalashnikov rifles, grenades, mines, and RPG rockets.

END OF THE BRIEF

GLOBAL DEFENCE SPENDING HITS 16-YEAR HIGH

A new report by Stockholm International Peace Research Institute shows global military spending surged to $2.9 trillion in 2025, the highest share of global GDP since 2009.

Highlights:
- Europe (+14%) and Asia (+8.1%) drove the spike
- Top spenders: U.S., China, Russia, Germany, India (58% of total)
- India spent ~$92.1B, up 8.9%

Rising tensions and ongoing conflicts are pushing nations toward long-term military expansion, with spending expected to keep climbing into 2026 and beyond.
